speaker: narrator
text: Three days had passed since the dragon's death, and Thornwick still hadn't stopped smoking.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin had spent most of that time in fever dreams, his body fighting the scale's invasion while his mind spiraled through visions he couldn't hold. Even asleep, pain was his pulse -- the scale burned, a low ember ache that never cooled.

speaker: narrator
text: The bonding hadn't been clean; it rewrote him from the inside out. He'd burned so hot that Vex packed him in snow from shaded hollows just to stop him smoking.

speaker: narrator
text: By the third morning, the fever broke.

speaker: narrator
text: The scale settled to its baseline: a thumb-length, hex-edged plate lying flush in his skin, translucent obsidian with ember-motes drifting inside, pulsing steady.

speaker: narrator
text: The filaments had spread -- not just toward wrist and elbow now, but creeping past the joint. Dark threads visible beneath skin that would never look quite normal again.

speaker: narrator
text: He flexed his fingers, testing. The embedded plate flexed with him. The filaments shifted like roots seeking deeper soil.

speaker: vex
text: "You look less on fire today,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said from the doorway of the half-collapsed inn where they'd taken shelter. Her eyes didn't match -- one gold-hazel, one storm-grey -- the only inheritance from a mother who'd spent her life studying magic she wasn't supposed to touch.

speaker: vex
text: "Improvement or just denial?"

speaker: narrator
text: . She held a clay cup that steamed in the morning chill.

speaker: vex
text: "A little."

speaker: narrator
text: The truth came easier after three days of the scale punishing every half-lie his fever-addled mind had tried.

speaker: vex
text: "Different now. Less like fire, more like... weight."

speaker: vex
text: "The veins are spreading."

speaker: narrator
text: Not a question. She'd been watching the progression, cataloging it with the same attention she gave potential threats and profitable opportunities.

speaker: narrator
text: He tugged his sleeve down. The cloth caught on the plate's raised edge -- a small, stupid reminder he would never hide it for long.

speaker: narrator
text: He'd tested it that morning out of stubbornness -- wrapped it, braced it, even tried steel.: linen wrappings had smoldered after an hour, and the leather bracer from the inn's abandoned supplies had conducted heat until it burned his skin, leaving blisters that still throbbed.

speaker: narrator
text: Metal was worse -- the scale had flared white-hot the moment steel touched it, as if offended by the attempt at concealment.

speaker: vex
text: "Right,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said, watching the sleeve ride back up.

speaker: vex
text: "Not ominous at all."

speaker: narrator
text: She handed him the cup -- broth made from dried vegetables and questionable meat, but hot and real.

speaker: vex
text: "Drink. You need to eat actual food today or you'll collapse halfway to wherever that magic compass in your arm wants to drag us."

speaker: narrator
text: He sipped. It tasted like survival -- not good, but good enough.

speaker: vex
text: "You stayed."

speaker: vex
text: "You didn't have to,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said, surprised how small his voice sounded.

speaker: vex
text: "Investment protection."

speaker: narrator
text: She settled against the opposite wall, her own cup cradled in scarred hands.

speaker: vex
text: "Spent three days keeping you alive, forcing water down your throat when you tried to bite me, and listening to you speak fluent Draconic in your sleep -- which was deeply unsettling, by the way. Would be wasteful to let you die now."

speaker: narrator
text: The scale stayed warm. No sting. Close enough to truth -- she had stayed for practical reasons, even if other reasons were growing beneath the surface.

speaker: vex
text: "The dreams,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said.

speaker: vex
text: "What did I say?"

speaker: vex
text: "Nothing I could understand. But you said it with authority."

speaker: narrator
text: Her mismatched eyes studied him.

speaker: vex
text: "And that thing -- "

speaker: narrator
text: a gesture at his arm

speaker: vex
text: " -- lit up the whole room every time you started. Like sleeping next to a forge that randomly decided to perform a light show."

speaker: narrator
text: He'd noticed. The scale answered emotion as much as magic: hesitation brought the white-hot sting; fever dreams brought the full prismatic display. He was learning to school his thoughts, to keep them surface-level and practical, to not think too deeply about what he'd become.

speaker: narrator
text: The scale warmed slightly. It knew what he was doing.

speaker: caelin
text: "We should go back,"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin said, pushing to his feet. His legs protested but held.

speaker: vex
text: "To the crater. I need to see it with clear eyes."

speaker: caelin
text: "Already scouted it twice."

speaker: narrator
text: But Vex stood as well, checking her daggers from habit.

speaker: vex
text: "Nothing's changed. Dragon's still dead and crystallized. Path still leads to the mountains. Village still looks like someone melted it into modern art."

speaker: caelin
text: "I need to see it anyway."

speaker: narrator
text: She shrugged.

speaker: vex
text: "Your funeral. Or mine, probably, since I'm following you."

speaker: narrator
text: They left the inn as morning light turned the transformed village into something almost beautiful. Glass-smooth walls caught sun and threw rainbows. Crystalline puddles reflected sky in fractured mirrors.

speaker: narrator
text: Even the destruction had an elegance to it -- the way catastrophe sometimes does, when you're far enough away from the dying.

speaker: narrator
text: People watched them pass. Fewer than before -- half the survivors had already left for Millbrook, unable to stay in a place that wore its trauma like decoration. Those who remained were the stubborn or the broken, the ones with nowhere else to go or reasons to stay that outweighed sense.

speaker: narrator
text: A woman carrying a child saw Caelin's exposed forearm and made a warding sign.

speaker: narrator
text: An old man simply stared, mouth working soundlessly.

speaker: vex
text: "They're afraid,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex murmured.

speaker: caelin
text: "I saved a child here."

speaker: vex
text: "You did. Then you went to meet a dragon and came back changed."

speaker: narrator
text: She kept her voice low.

speaker: caelin
text: "People fear what they don't understand. That -- "

speaker: narrator
text: a subtle gesture

speaker: vex
text: " -- they definitely don't understand."

speaker: narrator
text: The scale pulsed warm. She was right, as usual.

speaker: narrator
text: They followed the path of scorched hedge and melted stone toward the crater. The destruction formed a clear line -- deliberate, purposeful -- leading away from the village center. Morning mist clung to the hollows, making the landscape seem unreal, halfway between memory and present.

speaker: narrator
text: Where the hedgerow ended, the crater waited.

speaker: narrator
text: She was gone. Not crystallised, not preserved -- gone entirely. The glass floor held only the ghost of her outline, a perfect dragon-shaped shadow scorched into the obsidian where ten thousand years of history had finally settled and gone dark.

speaker: narrator
text: The ember-motes in Caelin's scale drifted a little faster as he slid down the slope. He wondered if they knew.

speaker: vex
text: "What are you looking for?"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex called from the rim.

speaker: caelin
text: "I don't know."

speaker: narrator
text: He crossed to the centre and crouched at the outline's edge. The glass was warm here -- just faintly, the last of it. His marked arm pulsed once in recognition, amber light catching nothing.

speaker: vex
text: "Thank you,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said quietly, not sure if it was gratitude or accusation. Maybe both.

speaker: narrator
text: The glass didn't answer.

speaker: narrator
text: Footsteps crunched above -- not Vex, different rhythm. Caelin turned, hand moving instinctively toward the sword he'd left at the inn.

speaker: narrator
text: A figure crested the crater's rim from the north side -- short, broad, trailing a small cloud of steam and the unmistakable tick-tick-whirr of clockwork in motion. Even at a distance, the mechanical clicking carried across the glass-smooth bowl with crystal clarity.

speaker: vex
text: "That's either the world's loudest thief,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex called down, daggers already in hand,

speaker: caelin
text: "or a very confused tinker."

speaker: narrator
text: The figure resolved as it descended: a dwarf, copper beard braided with gear-charms that glinted in the light, goggles pushed up on a wild-browed forehead revealing keen eyes beneath.

speaker: narrator
text: A pack that seemed more metal than leather rode his shoulders, bristling with tools and devices and things that hummed.

speaker: narrator
text: He stopped twenty feet away, tilted his head at Caelin like a bird examining something shiny, and beamed with the particular joy of someone proving themselves right after a long argument.

speaker: vex
text: "Aha!"

speaker: narrator
text: The dwarf's voice carried genuine delight.

speaker: caelin
text: "Knew the readings couldn't be wrong. Well, they're usually wrong, but not about this. Never about this."

speaker: narrator
text: He unslung a brass contraption from his shoulder -- a brass contraption of spinning dials and trembling needles that twisted light in uneasy patterns. He pointed it at Caelin.

speaker: narrator
text: The device screamed.

speaker: narrator
text: Not metaphorically -- literally shrieked, a high metallic wail that sent birds scattering from the crater's rim and made Caelin's teeth ache.

speaker: narrator
text: The dwarf slapped it. The screaming became sparking. He slapped it again. Silence, except for an ominous ticking that suggested the device was either thinking or plotting revenge.

speaker: vex
text: "Brilliant,"

speaker: narrator
text: he told the device with genuine affection. Then, to them:

speaker: caelin
text: "Thornik Bramblebrew. Artificer, engineer, and occasional brewer of exploding ale -- though that last bit's more accident than intention. You're the Ember-born, unless my instruments are lying. Which they don't, except when they do, but not about dragon-marks. Never about dragon-marks."

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik's eyes went wide behind his pushed-up goggles.

speaker: vex
text: "By my beard."

speaker: narrator
text: His voice dropped to something like reverence.

speaker: caelin
text: "It recognized my kit."

speaker: narrator
text: He took a step closer, goggles sliding down over his eyes with a mechanical snick. The lenses whirred, adjusting, magnifying.

speaker: vex
text: "May I? Grandfather wrote about this -- well, he wrote in blood, technically, which was dramatic even for him -- but if the resonance patterns match what he described, if the harmonic frequencies align, if the -- "

speaker: narrator
text: Vex shifted between them -- not blocking entirely, but making her presence felt.

speaker: caelin
text: "Maybe ask before reaching for things embedded in people's arms. Novel concept, I know."

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik held up both hands, device still humming.

speaker: vex
text: "Fair, fair. Wouldn't want to interfere with an active bonding anyway. Could cause sympathetic feedback loops."

speaker: narrator
text: He paused, considering.

speaker: caelin
text: "Or explosions. Fifty-fifty odds, really, and explosions are only fun when they're intentional."

speaker: caelin
text: "Comforting,"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin said, instinctively covering the scale with his other hand. It pulsed warmer, as if objecting to being hidden even by his own flesh.

speaker: vex
text: "What brings an artificer to a burned village?"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex asked, still wary, still positioned to strike if needed.

speaker: caelin
text: "Same thing that brought him, I'd wager."

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik's enthusiasm dimmed slightly, replaced by something heavier -- old grief wearing new clothes.

speaker: vex
text: "The dreams. My whole clan's been having them for months. Forges waking in the deep places where we sealed them. Constructs stirring in halls we'd forgotten. The old songs starting up again in chambers we'd filled with stone and regret."

speaker: narrator
text: He gestured at the dragon's crystalline corpse, at the transformed crater, at the morning light making art of catastrophe.

speaker: caelin
text: "Then three nights ago -- the exact night she died -- every piece of sensing equipment in Deepstone Hold went mad at once. Every dial spinning. Every alarm screaming. Every blessed instrument pointing here, at this spot, saying 'something fundamental just changed.'"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik pulled a water-stained journal from his coat -- leather gone soft with age and handling, pages brittle at the edges, stained with substances that might have been blood or oil or tears.

speaker: vex
text: "My grandfather died believing himself a fool for chasing legends. Spent his last gold on an expedition into the Emberpeaks, came back empty-handed and broken-spirited. Wrote in this -- in his own blood, mind you, because apparently sanity's optional in our family -- about hearing old songs, about seeing the pedestals of the Nine, about..."

speaker: narrator
text: He trailed off, shook his head, but his hands clutched the journal like it might escape.

speaker: caelin
text: "About warnings. Prices paid. Choices that haunt. He died thinking he'd failed. That he'd missed something vital. That he'd doomed us all by not understanding fast enough."

speaker: narrator
text: The dwarf's eyes met Caelin's, and the weight there aged him.

speaker: vex
text: "Point is, if he was right about any of it -- if even one page of his mad rambling was true -- then everything changes. The old alliances weren't myths. The Concord wasn't legend. And whatever's waking now..."

speaker: narrator
text: He glanced at the scale in Caelin's arm.

speaker: caelin
text: "It's already started."

speaker: narrator
text: The scale flared hot for just a moment -- not pain, but insistence. Share it. Tell him. The dragon had said to find others, to gather bearers. This dwarf had come seeking the same truth. His grandfather had died chasing it.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin hated that the scale was probably right.

speaker: vex
text: "The dragon spoke of nine relics,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said, the words feeling too large for the smoking crater and morning light.

speaker: caelin
text: "Forged in concordance. Nine bearers to claim them. And something beneath stone -- something called the Seal -- that has to stay sleeping."

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik's face cycled through several emotions in rapid succession: shock, vindication, terror, and finally settling on manic determination.

speaker: vex
text: "The Concord of Nine. Gods and ancestors and all the stone beneath, he was right."

speaker: narrator
text: He opened the journal with trembling hands, pages crackling.

speaker: caelin
text: "Grandfather wrote about it. The great alliance -- dragons and dwarves forging together, nine artifacts to maintain the balance, one for each school of magic. But there were warnings too. About prices. About sacrifices. About -- "

speaker: narrator
text: his voice dropped

speaker: vex
text: " -- about what happens if the balance breaks."

speaker: vex
text: "What happens?"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex asked, climbing down into the crater now, curiosity overriding caution.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik looked up from the journal, and his expression was bleak.

speaker: caelin
text: "He didn't write it. Just left blank pages with one phrase: 'May none live to learn the answer.'"

speaker: narrator
text: Silence stretched between them, broken only by the soft ticking of Thornik's devices and the distant sound of Thornwick's survivors going about the grim business of salvage and departure.

speaker: narrator
text: The scale pulsed steady in Caelin's arm -- counting heartbeats, marking time, waiting for whatever came next.

speaker: narrator
text: A sound from the crater's rim made them all turn -- the clink of metal on stone, deliberate and measured. Not the mechanical clicking of Thornik's devices but something heavier. Purposeful.

speaker: narrator
text: A horse stood at the crater's edge, steam rising from its flanks. Its rider dismounted with practiced ease, though Caelin noted the stiffness in her movements -- old injuries, poorly healed, the kind that never quite stopped aching.

speaker: narrator
text: She wore armor that had once been white and gold but now looked sun-bleached and road-worn, the kind of patina that came from months in the saddle without proper maintenance.

speaker: narrator
text: Not from neglect, he realized, but from lack of time -- or perhaps lack of caring about appearances when survival took precedence.

speaker: vex
text: "I'm looking for Elder Gareth,"

speaker: narrator
text: she called down, her voice carrying the formal cadence of someone used to announcing themselves in halls of power. Then her gaze landed on Caelin's exposed forearm -- the sleeve had ridden up again during his conversation with Thornik -- and she stopped mid-sentence.

speaker: narrator
text: The scale reacted immediately.

speaker: narrator
text: Not the white-hot sting of lies or the prismatic shimmer of wild magic. This was different -- a steady, golden-ember glow that Caelin had never seen before. The light pulsed from the embedded plate like a heartbeat made visible, warm and... welcoming? No, not quite. Recognizing.

speaker: narrator
text: The woman's hand moved to her sword hilt -- not in threat but in reflex -- and her palm began to glow. Soft silver-gold light flickered across her skin like a candle struggling in wind, uncertain but persistent.

speaker: caelin
text: "What are you?"

speaker: narrator
text: she asked, and the question held no accusation, only genuine need to understand.

speaker: caelin
text: "Cursed,"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin said, because it felt more honest than

speaker: vex
text: "chosen."

speaker: thornik
text: "Dragon-marked,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik added helpfully, his device already whirring as it tracked the interaction.

speaker: vex
text: "First verified case in three generations. Possibly longer. My readings indicate a stable harmonic resonance between -- "

speaker: thornik
text: "I wasn't asking you."

speaker: narrator
text: But her grip on the sword relaxed fractionally. The light in her palm didn't fade, but it steadied somewhat, silver and gold still dancing their uneasy dance but no longer quite so frantic.

speaker: narrator
text: She descended into the crater with care, testing each step on the glass slope before committing her weight. Up close, Caelin could see the exhaustion in her face -- the kind that went deeper than lack of sleep, that spoke of questions without answers and certainty turned to sand.

speaker: vex
text: "Serana Valeblade,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said, the formality a shield or perhaps just habit.

speaker: thornik
text: "Paladin of the Silver Dawn. I came to offer aid to the survivors, but..."

speaker: narrator
text: She looked around at the glass-fused crater, the crystalline dragon, the morning light making art of devastation.

speaker: vex
text: "This is beyond healing. This is divine reckoning."

speaker: vex
text: "Just dragon fire,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said from where she leaned against the crater wall, affecting casual despite the tension in her shoulders.

speaker: thornik
text: "Very enthusiastic dragon fire, admittedly, but nothing divine about it."

speaker: vex
text: "You're wrong."

speaker: narrator
text: Serana's voice carried absolute certainty -- the kind that came from hearing gods speak. Then that certainty cracked, and something raw showed through.

speaker: thornik
text: "At least... I think you're wrong. I used to be sure. Now..."

speaker: narrator
text: She closed her eyes briefly, and when she opened them, the exhaustion was even clearer.

speaker: vex
text: "Since the dragon's death my god speaks with two voices -- one preaching mercy, one sacrifice. I used to know which was truth. Now I don't."

speaker: narrator
text: She looked at Caelin, at the scale pulsing its steady golden rhythm in time with her flickering light.

speaker: thornik
text: "So I came seeking answers. Or at least clarity. Someone who could tell me if my god has fractured or if I have."

speaker: narrator
text: Her jaw set.

speaker: vex
text: "Instead I find... that."

speaker: narrator
text: The scale pulsed again -- ember and silver-gold, fire and holy radiance, beating in an alternating pattern like a shared heartbeat. One pulse of gold from the scale, one flicker of silver from her palm, back and forth in perfect synchronization.

speaker: thornik
text: "The gods know,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik breathed, watching the synchronized pulsing with wide eyes. His device had stopped its usual clicking and settled into a low, resonant hum.

speaker: vex
text: "They know what's waking. That's why her guidance splits -- divine will fracturing under the weight of impossible choices."

speaker: serana
text: "What choices?"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana demanded, her hand dropping from her sword to press against her chest where the Silver Dawn's symbol hung.

speaker: vex
text: "What could possibly justify telling a paladin to both protect and sacrifice? To both shepherd and... and cull?"

speaker: thornik
text: "The Concord,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik said quietly, and suddenly all the manic energy had drained from him, leaving only the weight of inherited knowledge.

speaker: vex
text: "Grandfather wrote about it in the margins. The old alliance required... balance. Sometimes protection meant destruction. Sometimes healing meant cutting away the infected flesh before it could spread."

speaker: narrator
text: He looked at Serana with something like sympathy.

speaker: thornik
text: "Your god isn't fracturing, lady paladin. Your god is preparing you for choices where both commands will be true at once. Where protecting some means sacrificing others. Where keeping the greater balance requires breaking lesser ones."

speaker: serana
text: "That's not -- "

speaker: narrator
text: Serana started, then stopped. The silver-gold light in her palm flared brighter for a moment, as if in confirmation, and she looked at it with something between betrayal and understanding.

speaker: thornik
text: "That's not the Silver Dawn's teaching. We don't trade lives like currency. We don't choose who deserves protection and who doesn't."

speaker: vex
text: "Maybe you didn't have to before,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said, surprisingly gentle.

speaker: thornik
text: "Maybe the choices were always clear enough that mercy and pragmatism pointed the same direction. But if something's changing -- if something sealed is waking -- maybe the easy choices are over."

speaker: narrator
text: The scale pulsed warmer in Caelin's arm, but didn't sting. Truth, whether welcome or not.

speaker: narrator
text: Serana was quiet for a long moment, staring at her glowing palm, at the scale answering it with golden light, at the dragon's crystalline corpse bearing silent witness to transformation and consequence.

speaker: serana
text: "What did she tell you?"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana asked finally, looking at Caelin.

speaker: thornik
text: "The dragon. Before she died. What did she say was coming that could fracture divine guidance itself?"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin flexed his marked arm, feeling the filaments shift beneath skin, the embedded plate counting heartbeats with patient precision.

speaker: serana
text: "She said nine relics were forged to maintain balance. That nine bearers are needed to claim them. And that something called the Tenth -- something sealed beneath stone -- has to stay sleeping."

speaker: narrator
text: He met her eyes.

speaker: thornik
text: "She said if the Nine fail, if the balance breaks, then what's buried will wake. And it's wrong for the breathing world."

speaker: serana
text: "The Tenth,"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana repeated, and her silver-gold light guttered like a candle in wind.

speaker: thornik
text: "My prayers this morning... when I asked what I should do, what path I should follow... the answer came in two voices."

speaker: narrator
text: She swallowed hard.

speaker: serana
text: "One said find the Nine, keep the chain. The other said some chains deserve breaking."

speaker: thornik
text: "Which voice was louder?"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik asked.

speaker: serana
text: "Neither. Both. They spoke over each other, equally strong, equally certain."

speaker: narrator
text: She looked lost.

speaker: thornik
text: "How do I serve my god when my god speaks with two mouths?"

speaker: vex
text: "You choose,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said bluntly.

speaker: thornik
text: "You pick which voice sounds more like the deity you swore to serve, and you follow that one. The other voice -- "

speaker: narrator
text: she shrugged

speaker: vex
text: " -- might not be your god at all. Might be something else trying to sound like it."

speaker: narrator
text: The scale flared white-hot against Caelin's skin -- sudden, searing. He gasped, cradling his arm.

speaker: thornik
text: "What?"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex spun toward him.

speaker: vex
text: "What did I say?"

speaker: thornik
text: "Not you."

speaker: narrator
text: He gritted his teeth against the pain.

speaker: vex
text: "The scale. It's warning -- "

speaker: narrator
text: The obsidian plate shifted, and for the first time since bonding, black-violet bled through the translucent surface like ink in water. The ember-motes inside swirled faster, agitated, and the pulse went irregular -- fast, then slow, then hard.

speaker: thornik
text: "Corruption warning,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik said, his device suddenly screaming again. He slapped it silent and checked the dials with growing alarm.

speaker: vex
text: "Three signatures. No, four. Strong ones. Closing fast from different vectors."

speaker: thornik
text: "What are they?"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana's hand went back to her sword, and this time she drew it. Silver-gold light cascaded down the blade like liquid sunrise.

speaker: vex
text: "If I'm reading this right?"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik's voice had lost all its cheer.

speaker: thornik
text: "Whatever was drawn here after the dragon died. Whatever's been hunting these mountains for something it can finally smell."

speaker: narrator
text: The scale's black-violet bloom intensified. The irregular pulse hammered against Caelin's nerves -- wrong wrong wrong in a rhythm that made his teeth ache.

speaker: vex
text: "And these readings..."

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik tapped his device as if hoping it was malfunctioning.

speaker: thornik
text: "These don't match anything living."

speaker: narrator
text: A scream rose from the direction of the village -- sharp, terrified, and cut short with sickening abruptness.

speaker: narrator
text: They ran.

speaker: narrator
text: Boots on glass, then dirt, then cobblestone still warm from dragon-fire three days old. The transformed village seemed to resist their passage, crystalline surfaces slick and treacherous, fused walls creating dead ends where paths used to run.

speaker: narrator
text: The scale's black-violet warning pulsed harder with each step, and Caelin felt something he hadn't felt since the bonding: fear not his own. The scale was afraid. Or warning him to be. Or both.

speaker: narrator
text: They burst into the village square and stopped.

speaker: narrator
text: A figure stood over the fallen -- someone Caelin recognized, vaguely, from three days ago. A trader, maybe, or a traveler who'd come seeking news of the dragon and found catastrophe instead. The body was intact, clothes recognizable, face still human.

speaker: narrator
text: But the eyes were wrong.

speaker: narrator
text: Black from lid to lid. No white, no iris -- just obsidian darkness that reflected no light and seemed to drink the morning sun.

speaker: narrator
text: And the shadow.

speaker: narrator
text: Gods, the shadow.

speaker: narrator
text: It moved independent of the figure, stretching and bending in directions that had nothing to do with where the light fell. Tendrils of darkness writhed around the figure's feet, tasting the air like serpents, reaching toward the living with obvious hunger.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik's device had stopped screaming and started weeping -- a low, mournful tone that resonated in Caelin's chest, in his bones, in the scale's frantic black-violet pulse.

speaker: narrator
text: The corrupted figure turned toward them with liquid grace, head tilting at an angle just wrong enough to trigger every instinct that said predator.

speaker: narrator
text: When it spoke, three voices emerged braided together -- the person it had been, something ancient and cold, and underneath both, the sound like stone grinding stone.

speaker: vex
text: "Ember-born,"

speaker: narrator
text: it said, and smiled with too many teeth.

speaker: thornik
text: "The Seal remembers. The Seal hungers."

speaker: narrator
text: Two more emerged from between buildings -- moving with that same wrong fluidity, shadows writhing like living things, black eyes drinking light.

speaker: vex
text: "Behind the wagons!"

speaker: narrator
text: Elder Gareth's voice bellowed from across the square.

speaker: thornik
text: "Get the children behind the wagons!"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana's sword flared brighter, silver-gold light casting hard shadows.

speaker: vex
text: "Positions!"

speaker: narrator
text: The scale's black-violet bloom filled Caelin's entire forearm, pulsing so hard it hurt. And beneath the warning, beneath the fear, something else:

speaker: narrator
text: Purpose.

speaker: narrator
text: The Dragon's Ember stirred in his chest -- not called, not yet, but ready. Waiting for the moment when warning became action, when fear became fuel.

speaker: narrator
text: The shadow-thralls took a step forward, moving in perfect synchronization.

speaker: vex
text: "Three of them,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said, daggers appearing in her hands like magic tricks.

speaker: thornik
text: "Four of us. I've had worse odds."

speaker: serana
text: "Five,"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana corrected, stepping forward with her blade held steady despite the fear Caelin could see in her eyes.

speaker: thornik
text: "Stay behind me."

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik set his pack down with surprising gentleness, and when he stood again, he held a fat brass cylinder in one hand and had reached back to unhook the twin axes from his shoulders.

speaker: serana
text: "Right then,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said, and his voice held something almost like joy beneath the terror.

speaker: thornik
text: "Let's see if Grandfather's warnings were complete. Avert your eyes, lovely people."

speaker: narrator
text: The shadow-thralls smiled their too-wide smiles and charged.

speaker: narrator
text: The fight was short and ugly.

speaker: narrator
text: Serana hit first -- training overriding fear, muscle memory carrying her forward when thought might have frozen her. Her blade traced an arc of silver-gold light that cut through the air like a falling star, and where it met shadow-tether, the darkness screamed.

speaker: narrator
text: Not metaphorically. The shadows shrieked with voices that shouldn't exist, writhing away from the divine radiance like living things recoiling from fire. The thrall she'd struck staggered, its movements suddenly uncoordinated, as if strings had been cut.

speaker: serana
text: "The light severs them!"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana called, her voice steady despite the horror.

speaker: thornik
text: "Aim for the shadows, not the bodies!"

speaker: narrator
text: The corrupted figure knitted itself back together wrong -- limbs jerking, spine bending in directions spines shouldn't bend -- but it was slower now, damaged, the black in its eyes boiling like tar on summer stone.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik didn't wait for a second demonstration. He snapped a rune on the brass cylinder's base and hurled it into the gap between two thralls. It clattered across cobblestone with a sound like manic laughter -- tick-tick-tick -- and detonated.

speaker: narrator
text: The explosion wasn't fire. It was force -- a concussive whump that picked up loose debris and flung it outward in a perfect sphere. Shockwave, grit, and a flash bright enough to paint shadows on walls. The thralls flinched back, shadows fragmenting, bodies stumbling.

speaker: serana
text: "Ha!"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik bellowed, already reaching for his axes.

speaker: thornik
text: "That's the good batch! Patent pending! Please don't steal my design!"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex didn't waste breath on commentary. She slid in low while the thralls were still reeling from Thornik's blast, moving with the fluid economy of someone who'd learned to fight in alleys where hesitation meant death. Two quick strikes -- precise, surgical, merciless.

speaker: narrator
text: Hamstring on the first. The thrall's leg buckled, dropping it to one knee.

speaker: narrator
text: Kidney on the second as it turned toward her. The blade found the gap between ribs like it had eyes of its own.

speaker: serana
text: "Less talking,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said, already spinning away from the third thrall's grasping reach.

speaker: thornik
text: "More dying. Preferably them, not us."

speaker: narrator
text: The third thrall lunged past Serana's guard, moving faster than something that broken should manage, straight for Caelin.

speaker: narrator
text: The scale ignited.

speaker: narrator
text: Not with warning this time. With demand. The black-violet corruption bloom inverted instantly -- from warning to weapon, fear to fuel. Heat surged up Caelin's arm, into his chest, and the Dragon's Ember answered.

speaker: narrator
text: He raised his right hand -- instinct, not thought -- and called fire.

speaker: narrator
text: Dragonfire came.

speaker: narrator
text: Not the careful flames he'd shaped to save Mira, but something older. Rawer. A focused cone of heat that turned the air glass-hot and sent the thrall reeling backward, skin blistering, shadow shrieking as it burned.

speaker: narrator
text: The thrall's movements became frantic, uncoordinated. Whatever was puppeting the body didn't understand fire -- or perhaps remembered it too well, knew it from before stone and sealing and the long dark beneath mountains.

speaker: narrator
text: Then the cost hit.

speaker: narrator
text: The scale flared white-hot -- pain branding every nerve -- then cycled through prismatic light to an angry red that hammered with his pulse. Vision tunneled, breath coming ragged.

speaker: narrator
text: He'd pushed too hard, used too much, let the Ember have too free a rein.

speaker: narrator
text: Pain tore up his arm in waves, each pulse worse than the last. His knees started to buckle.

speaker: narrator
text: Serana stepped through him -- literally shoulder-checked him backward out of the line of engagement -- and brought her blade down in a clean arc that finished the staggered thrall.

speaker: narrator
text: Silver-gold light washed through the thing's center, and it fell in pieces that began dissolving into smoke before they hit ground.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik met the last thrall with a roar and axes -- no gadgets now, just dwarf-forged steel and ancestral fury. The first axe bit through a corrupted shoulder. The second took it in the side.

speaker: serana
text: "Back to the pit with ye!"

speaker: narrator
text: The thrall collapsed, shadows fragmenting like shattered glass.

speaker: narrator
text: They stood in the scorched square, panting, as silence fell heavy and absolute. Around them: smoke, crying from somewhere distant, the groan of a damaged wagon axle, the smell of charred flesh and something else -- something chemical and wrong, like copper mixed with rot.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin sank to his knees, cradling his marked arm. The scale pulsed its angry red throb, counting the cost of power used too desperately. Each pulse sent fresh agony through the filaments.

speaker: narrator
text: He'd known there would be pain -- the dragon had warned him -- but knowing and experiencing were different countries entirely.

speaker: serana
text: "Breathe,"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana said, kneeling beside him but not touching -- as if she understood that contact would only make it worse.

speaker: thornik
text: "The backlash will fade. Just breathe through it."

speaker: serana
text: "How do you know?"

speaker: narrator
text: he managed through gritted teeth.

speaker: thornik
text: "I don't. But it's what I'd want someone to tell me."

speaker: narrator
text: Her honesty was almost comforting.

speaker: narrator
text: Vex was already checking the fallen bodies -- quick, professional, making sure the dead stayed dead.

speaker: serana
text: "These were people,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said, and something in her voice was harder than usual.

speaker: thornik
text: "Probably from the refugee caravans. Maybe from Thornwick itself."

speaker: thornik
text: "Not people anymore,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik said grimly, wiping his axes on a piece of torn cloth. He bent to examine his device, which had finally stopped its mournful weeping.

speaker: serana
text: "Shadow corruption. Seal-touched, if Grandfather's notes are accurate. The thing beneath uses them like... like tools. Puppets with meat."

speaker: narrator
text: He looked up, and his expression was bleak.

speaker: thornik
text: "And these weren't scouts. My readings show they were lures."

speaker: serana
text: "Lures for what?"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana asked, though her tone suggested she already knew the answer wouldn't be good.

speaker: thornik
text: "For whatever's coming next. To see who answers. To see who can fight."

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik tapped a dial that still spun lazily.

speaker: serana
text: "And we just showed it exactly what we're capable of."

speaker: vex
text: "Meaning more will come,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex finished. She stood, daggers still in hand, scanning the ruined square like she expected attack from every shadow.

speaker: serana
text: "They always do. News travels fast among monsters."

speaker: narrator
text: Elder Gareth emerged from behind a wagon, two children clutched in the crooks of his arms, his face grey with exhaustion and fear. Other survivors followed -- perhaps twenty in total, those who hadn't fled yet or couldn't, clinging to each other and what remained of their home.

speaker: gareth
text: "We cannot hold this place,"

speaker: narrator
text: Gareth said, and the words seemed to cost him.

speaker: serana
text: "If they followed the dragon's fire, if they're drawn by what you carry -- "

speaker: narrator
text: his eyes found Caelin's still-glowing scale

speaker: gareth
text: " -- they'll follow you. And we cannot survive another attack."

speaker: narrator
text: The flame-path flared at the edge of Caelin's vision, suddenly visible despite the pain, tugging like a hooked nerve toward the Emberpeaks. Had it always pulled this insistently, or had claiming his attention in front of threats made it more demanding?

speaker: caelin
text: "You want us to leave,"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin said. Not a question.

speaker: gareth
text: "I want you to lead them away."

speaker: narrator
text: Gareth's voice was firm despite the tremor in his hands.

speaker: caelin
text: "Draw whatever's hunting you toward the mountains, away from the caravan route to Millbrook. Give us time to evacuate what's left."

speaker: serana
text: "That's -- "

speaker: narrator
text: Serana started, then stopped. Her silver-gold light flickered uncertainly.

speaker: caelin
text: "That's asking them to walk into danger to protect strangers."

speaker: serana
text: "I'm asking them to do what they were going to do anyway."

speaker: narrator
text: Gareth looked at each of them in turn.

speaker: caelin
text: "The mark pulls him. The path leads to the mountains. His purpose isn't here -- it's forward, toward whatever the dragon bound him to find."

speaker: narrator
text: His gaze settled on Caelin.

speaker: serana
text: "Am I wrong?"

speaker: narrator
text: The scale pulsed warm agreement. No sting. The old man was right -- Caelin would follow the flame-path regardless. The only question was whether he'd lead danger toward innocents or away from them.

speaker: caelin
text: "No,"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin admitted, pushing to his feet despite the lingering pain.

speaker: serana
text: "You're not wrong."

speaker: caelin
text: "Then go. Today. Now, before more come."

speaker: narrator
text: Gareth shifted the children in his arms, their faces buried against his shoulders.

speaker: serana
text: "We'll head south to Millbrook. You head east to the Emberpeaks. Separate paths mean separate targets."

speaker: vex
text: "He's not wrong,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said quietly.

speaker: serana
text: "Strategically sound. Cold, but sound."

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik was already shouldering his pack, checking straps and buckles with practiced efficiency.

speaker: vex
text: "Been planning to head that direction anyway. My instruments have been screaming about something in those mountains since this all started. Might as well investigate with company instead of dying alone and unmourned."

speaker: serana
text: "I came seeking answers,"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana said slowly, her hand on her sword hilt, her divine light still flickering between commands.

speaker: vex
text: "Perhaps the answers aren't here. Perhaps they're where you're going."

speaker: narrator
text: She looked at Caelin.

speaker: serana
text: "If my god's guidance splits over the Nine, then maybe I need to see the Nine myself to understand which voice to follow."

speaker: vex
text: "So we're doing this,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said.

speaker: serana
text: "Following the glowing destiny road into certain danger based on a dead dragon's cryptic instructions. Grand."

speaker: narrator
text: The scale pulsed steady now -- the angry red had faded to something closer to its baseline coal-glow, though the filaments still ached.

speaker: narrator
text: The black-violet corruption warning had dissipated entirely with the thralls' destruction, but Caelin knew it would return. The Seal knew they were coming now. It had tasted their power and found them wanting to test again.

speaker: caelin
text: "We leave within the hour,"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin said, and even he heard how his voice had changed -- less question, more command. The scale approved with a pulse of warmth.

speaker: serana
text: "Gather what supplies you can. Travel light but prepare for weather and combat both."

speaker: vex
text: "Listen to him giving orders,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex murmured, but there was something almost fond beneath the sarcasm.

speaker: serana
text: "Three days with a magic tattoo and he thinks he's a commander."

speaker: thornik
text: "The mark commands,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik said, surprisingly serious.

speaker: serana
text: "He's just learning to speak its language."

speaker: narrator
text: They dispersed to prepare -- Thornik to check his devices, Serana to tend the wounded one last time before departing, Vex to

speaker: thornik
text: "acquire"

speaker: narrator
text: whatever supplies the village could spare and wouldn't miss.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin stood in the square as survivors began loading wagons, the work of evacuation resuming around him like he was a stone in a stream. He looked at his marked arm, at the scale that had settled back to its steady pulse, at the filaments mapping his skin in dark tracery.

speaker: narrator
text: Mira appeared at his elbow -- silent as a ghost, still clutching his ruined cloak from three days ago. She looked up at him with eyes too old for her age.

speaker: serana
text: "You're leaving,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said.

speaker: thornik
text: "I have to."

speaker: serana
text: "Because of what the dragon gave you."

speaker: narrator
text: Not a question. She reached out and touched the scale with one small fingertip, and it pulsed gentle warmth -- the first time since the fight it hadn't caused pain.

speaker: caelin
text: "Will you keep the rhyme safe?"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin asked.

speaker: serana
text: "The one the dragon sang?"

speaker: caelin
text: "Every word,"

speaker: narrator
text: she promised.

speaker: serana
text: "And when you come back, I'll say it for you again so you remember why you left."

speaker: caelin
text: "If I come back."

speaker: narrator
text: The scale stung. White-hot, sharp, brief.

speaker: narrator
text: Mira's eyes widened, but she didn't pull her finger away.

speaker: serana
text: "It wants you to tell the truth,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said with a child's directness.

speaker: caelin
text: "It does."

speaker: serana
text: "Then tell the truth."

speaker: narrator
text: He knelt so they were eye-level.

speaker: caelin
text: "I don't know if I'll come back. The path is long and leads to dangerous places. But if I do what I'm meant to do -- if I find what I'm meant to find -- then places like Thornwick will be safer. Children won't lose their homes. Dragons won't come searching."

speaker: narrator
text: The scale stayed warm. Truth accepted.

speaker: narrator
text: Mira hugged him with fierce strength, then pulled back and pressed something into his hand -- a river stone, worn smooth, with a hole through its center.

speaker: serana
text: "For luck. Papa said stones with holes are lucky because they've been blessed by water long enough to let light through."

speaker: caelin
text: "Thank you,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said, closing his fingers around it. The stone was warm from her pocket, smooth and real and ordinary in the best way.

speaker: narrator
text: She ran back to the wagons before he could say more, and he watched her go -- another person he was leaving behind, another reason to succeed written in the set of a child's shoulders.

speaker: narrator
text: An hour later, they assembled at the eastern edge of the village where the flame-path pulled strongest. Five of them now -- Caelin, Vex, Thornik, Serana, and their packs of hastily gathered supplies. Behind them, Thornwick smoked and evacuated.

speaker: narrator
text: Ahead, the Emberpeaks rose blue and distant and patient.

speaker: narrator
text: The flame-path stretched clear and bright in Caelin's sight, unmistakable as a road paved with light only he could see.

speaker: vex
text: "So we just walk toward the mountains,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said.

speaker: caelin
text: "Following something invisible."

speaker: vex
text: "That's the plan,"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin confirmed.

speaker: caelin
text: "Terrible plan."

speaker: narrator
text: But she adjusted her pack and checked her daggers one more time.

speaker: vex
text: "I've followed worse."

speaker: narrator
text: Serana touched the symbol at her throat, lips moving in silent prayer. When she opened her eyes, the silver-gold light flickered but held steady.

speaker: caelin
text: "Together then. For whatever answers wait ahead."

speaker: thornik
text: "For answers,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik agreed.

speaker: caelin
text: "And for Grandfather's vindication, may the old fool rest easier knowing he wasn't mad after all."

speaker: narrator
text: They set out together, leaving Thornwick's smoke behind. The flame-path led them across fields where ash still drifted, toward foothills that climbed into mysteries older than kingdoms.

speaker: narrator
text: The scale pulsed its steady rhythm against Caelin's skin -- counting breaths, marking steps, pulling him inexorably forward.

speaker: narrator
text: Behind them, Elder Gareth watched from the last wagon, one hand raised in blessing or farewell.

speaker: narrator
text: Ahead, the mountains waited with patient, geological indifference.

speaker: vex
text: "Five people,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said, not quite to anyone.

speaker: caelin
text: "Following a road only one of us can see."

speaker: thornik
text: "Seven,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik corrected, patting his pack.

speaker: caelin
text: "Grandfather's notes count."

speaker: narrator
text: Nobody argued. The flame-path led on. They followed.
